summary_one_line: Input must form a well-structured protocol request (pop/://, GHTTP/, CRLF headers) driving a connection-reuse transfer so bytecount+headerbytecount != 0.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid protocol request carrying recognizable literal tokens (a `pop` scheme prefix at offset 0x06, `://`, a `GHTTP/` request-line fragment at offset 0x24, and CRLF-delimited header text like `Connect`/`Set-Cookie`/`Transfer`) plus a header/port preamble (`9001`, `850`) so the transfer reads body+header bytes and `data->req.bytecount + data->req.headerbytecount` is non-zero, taking the FALSE side at transfer.c:1866.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds are 112-130 bytes and contain the literal request scaffold — `70 6f 70` (pop) at 0x06, `3a 2f 2f` (://), `47 48 54 54 50 2f` (GHTTP/) at 0x24, and `0d 0a` CRLF header text (Connect/Set-Cookie/Transfer) — so a real transfer accrues bytes and the F side (bytecount+headerbytecount != 0) executes 1690/1672 times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds are short (35-73 bytes) noise dominated by `%` (0x25) and `A` (0x41) fill with no `://`, no `GHTTP/`, and no CRLF header block; the transfer reads zero body+header bytes so bytecount+headerbytecount==0 stays TRUE and the F side is never reached (hitcount 0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc run I2SRandReplace: the CmpLogObserver captures the operands of curl's protocol string/integer comparisons (scheme match, request-line literal, header tokens) and the mutator substitutes the expected operand into the input at matching offsets, synthesizing the `pop`/`GHTTP/`/CRLF scaffold that naive and value_profile cannot stumble onto by havoc byte mutation alone. The lineage of a winning cmplog seed contains I2S-floor (dash) ancestor rows, exclusive to cmplog/vpc in this canonical set.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of a compound condition gated on bytecount+headerbytecount being zero.
Both decisive pairs differ only by the I2S technique and the winner resolves 10/10 while the loser blocks 10/10.
The winning side is hit ~1690 times by the winner and 0 times by the loser.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=1690  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds carry a structured request with pop scheme, :// and a GHTTP/ request-line fragment plus CRLF header text.
Winner seed contains the GHTTP/ request-line literal followed by header text and CRLF Connect.
Loser seeds are short 35-byte inputs dominated by percent and A filler with no protocol scaffold.
At the request-line offset the winner concentrates on the GHTTP literal bytes while the loser is scattered non-protocol bytes.
At the loser-blocking direction the true side (bytecount+headerbytecount==0) is taken while the winner takes the false side.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which reads the CmpLog buffer and substitutes the matching operand into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so the vpc>vp pair isolates the same I2S substitution effect.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 470 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 00f515acb6373e33`; the 21-level ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 19 and 20, the cmplog/vpc-exclusive lower-bound signal of an I2SRandReplace find.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the gated condition (bytecount+headerbytecount != 0) is reachable by random byte input WITHOUT any protocol-literal comparison (no pop/://, no request-line literal, no header tokens) yet cmplog still resolves it faster than naive — showing the divergence is not driven by I2S operand substitution of those literals.

weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from the protocol literals (pop/GHTTP/, CRLF headers) to a non-zero bytecount+headerbytecount is inferred from source semantics, not directly observed in the seeds as a counter value.
